codeReview.summary: Reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 1fe2a81589f3b139ea85f0aaf76555d4dee3b893. The feature adds a polling-based relay event feed plus auto-detach/lightweight notification handling, but it introduces a blocking multi-user isolation regression by broadcasting session activity to every previously seen chat, and it also misses the exact auto-detach message text requested in the feature description.

issues.description: The lightweight notification path loops over every entry in `lastSeenChats` and sends `[session-name]` turn/input alerts to all of them unless they are currently attached to that same session. In a multi-user bot this leaks one user's session activity to unrelated users, which breaks the existing isolation contract (`validation-contract.md:206-207`) and the architecture's per-user attach model (`.factory/library/architecture.md:90,159,161`).

The auto-detach notice is emitted as `Auto-detached: local input detected.` with a trailing period, but the feature description explicitly asks for `Auto-detached: local input detected`. The new tests codify the punctuated variant instead of the requested string, so the implementation is slightly off-spec at the user-visible text boundary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared docs do not clearly spell out how lightweight notifications should be scoped in the multi-user bot. The architecture and validation contract talk about per-user attachment/isolation, but the feature description only says to notify on 'any registered session', which leaves room for an implementation that broadcasts other users' session activity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:90,159,161 describes per-user attachment; validation-contract.md:206-207 requires multi-user isolation; features.json:701 and 710-713 describe lightweight notifications without a user-scoping rule; bot/src/bot-service.ts:301-306 therefore broadcasts to every `lastSeenChats` entry.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 1fe2a81589f3b139ea85f0aaf76555d4dee3b893. The relay event polling and auto-detach plumbing are present, but the notification fan-out currently violates multi-user isolation by exposing other sessions' activity to every previously seen chat, and the auto-detach notice text also differs slightly from the requested string.
